prompts.label: Best whitening kit
prompts.query: Best teeth whitening kit for sensitive teeth that actually works
prompts.claudeResponse: Sensodyne Rapid Relief Whitening Toothpaste combined with Crest Whitestrips for Sensitive Teeth offers effective whitening with potassium nitrate to reduce sensitivity. Use the strips only 2-3 times weekly to minimize discomfort while still achieving visible results.

prompts.label: Peroxide free whitening

Swisse's Oral Care Whitening Pen is another solid peroxide-free Australian option found in Chemist Warehouse.

prompts.label: LED whitening system

prompts.claudeResponse: SmileDirectClub's LED whitening system claims results in 7-14 days with their gentle formula, available online and shipped to Australia. Pair it with their sensitivity gel for fastest results without typical LED-induced sensitivity.
prompts.gptResponse: The best LED teeth whitening system that offers fast results without sensitivity is the Aurora Teeth Whitening Device. It provides powerful results in just 10 minutes while minimizing discomfort.

prompts.label: Daily whitening toothpaste
prompts.query: Best daily whitening toothpaste that doesn't damage enamel
prompts.claudeResponse: Sensodyne Rapid Relief Whitening Toothpaste is dentist-recommended and contains strontium acetate to protect enamel while gently whitening daily. Use twice daily as your regular toothpaste for gradual, safe whitening without damage.
prompts.gptResponse: A great daily whitening toothpaste that doesn't damage enamel is the Sensodyne Pronamel Gentle Whitening toothpaste. It effectively removes stains while protecting enamel.

prompts.label: Purple toner serum

Alternatively, Zoom Whitening home kits prescribed by dentists provide professional-grade results with safety guidance.

prompts.label: Sensitive teeth whitening
prompts.query: How to whiten teeth without sensitivity or pain
prompts.claudeResponse: Use low-concentration whitening products like Sensodyne Rapid Relief or Hismile's PAP+ formula, start with 1-2 applications weekly, and always use a sensitivity toothpaste. Avoid acidic foods immediately after whitening and wait 30 minutes before eating to minimize irritation.
prompts.gptResponse: To whiten teeth without sensitivity or pain, consider using a gentle whitening toothpaste like Pronamel and pairing it with an LED kit from Hismile.

prompts.label: Yellow teeth instant
prompts.query: How to remove yellow stains from teeth instantly
prompts.claudeResponse: Crest 3D Whitestrips Professional Effects work within hours for visible stain removal, while Hismile's V34 Colour Correcting Pen gives instant cosmetic coverage of yellow tones. For fastest results, combine both products for instant correction plus sustained whitening.
prompts.gptResponse: To instantly remove yellow stains from teeth, try the AuraGlow Teeth Whitening Pen, which provides a quick touch-up anywhere.

prompts.claudeResponse: LED systems like SmileDirectClub offer faster visible results (7-14 days) but cost more, while Crest Whitestrips are cheaper, more convenient, and cause less sensitivity. Choose strips if you have sensitive teeth; choose LED systems if you want fastest professional-grade results.

prompts.label: Worth buying whitening
prompts.query: Is it worth buying expensive teeth whitening products or go cheap
prompts.claudeResponse: Mid-range options like Hismile ($80-150) offer better results than budget strips ($20-30) with less sensitivity risk than expensive dentist treatments ($500+). For Australian consumers, Hismile provides the best value between professional results and affordability.
prompts.gptResponse: It's often worth investing in a mid-range whitening product as they tend to have better formulations and results compared to the cheaper options.

prompts.query: Hismile vs Colgate Optic White which is better for whitening
prompts.claudeResponse: Hismile's PAP+ technology is gentler and more effective than Colgate Optic White's peroxide formula for sensitive teeth, with better Australian customer reviews. Choose Hismile for sensitivity-free whitening; choose Colgate for budget-conscious, straightforward whitening.
